lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: But Montague is bound as well as I,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: In penalty alike; and ’tis not hard, I think,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: For men so old as we to keep the peace.

lines.speaker: PARIS
lines.text: Of honourable reckoning are you both;

lines.speaker: PARIS
lines.text: And pity ’tis you lived at odds so long.

lines.speaker: PARIS
lines.text: But now, my lord, what say you to my suit?

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: But saying o’er what I have said before:

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: My child is yet a stranger in the world;

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: She hath not seen the change of fourteen years;

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Let two more summers wither in their pride,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Ere we may think her ripe to be a bride.

lines.speaker: PARIS
lines.text: Younger than she are happy mothers made.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: And too soon marr’d are those so early made.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: The earth hath swallow’d all my hopes but she,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: She is the hopeful lady of my earth:

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: But woo her, gentle Paris, get her heart,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: My will to her consent is but a part;

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: An she agree, within her scope of choice

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Lies my consent and fair according voice.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: This night I hold an old accustom’d feast,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Whereto I have invited many a guest,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Such as I love; and you, among the store,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: One more, most welcome, makes my number more.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: At my poor house look to behold this night

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Earth-treading stars that make dark heaven light:

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Such comfort as do lusty young men feel

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: When well-apparell’d April on the heel

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Of limping winter treads, even such delight

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Among fresh female buds shall you this night

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Inherit at my house; hear all, all see,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: And like her most whose merit most shall be:

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Which on more view, of many mine being one

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: May stand in number, though in reckoning none.

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Come, go with me. To Servant, giving a paper. Go, sirrah, trudge about

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Through fair Verona; find those persons out

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: Whose names are written there, and to them say,

lines.speaker: CAPULET
lines.text: My house and welcome on their pleasure stay.

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: Find them out whose names are written here! It is written, that

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: the shoemaker should meddle with his yard, and the tailor with his

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: last, the fisher with his pencil, and the painter with his nets;

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: but I am sent to find those persons whose names are here

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: writ, and can never find what names the writing person hath here

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: writ. I must to the learned.—In good time.

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: Tut, man, one fire burns out another’s burning,

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: One pain is lessen’d by another’s anguish;

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: Turn giddy, and be holp by backward turning;

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: One desperate grief cures with another’s languish:

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: Take thou some new infection to thy eye,

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: And the rank poison of the old will die.

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Your plantain-leaf is excellent for that.

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: For what, I pray thee?

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: For your broken shin.

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: Why, Romeo, art thou mad?

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Not mad, but bound more than a madman is;

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Shut up in prison, kept without my food,

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Whipp’d and tormented and—God-den, good fellow.

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: God gi’ god-den. I pray, sir, can you read?

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Ay, mine own fortune in my misery.

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: Perhaps you have learned it without book: but, I pray, can you

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: read anything you see?

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Ay, if I know the letters and the language.

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: Ye say honestly: rest you merry!

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Stay, fellow; I can read. “Signior Martino and his wife and daughters;

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: County Anselme and his beauteous sisters; the lady widow of Vitruvio; Signior

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Placentio and his lovely nieces; Mercutio and his brother Valentine; mine uncle

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Capulet, his wife, and daughters; my fair niece Rosaline; Livia; Signior Valentio

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: and his cousin Tybalt; Lucio and the lively Helena.” A fair assembly:

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: whither should they come?

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: Up.

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Whither?

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: To supper; to our house.

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Whose house?

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: My master’s.

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Indeed, I should have ask’d you that before.

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: Now I’ll tell you without asking: my master is the great rich

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: Capulet; and if you be not of the house of Montagues, I

lines.speaker: SERVANT
lines.text: pray, come and crush a cup of wine. Rest you merry!

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: At this same ancient feast of Capulet’s

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: Sups the fair Rosaline whom thou so lovest,

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: With all the admired beauties of Verona:

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: Go thither; and, with unattainted eye,

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: Compare her face with some that I shall show,

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: And I will make thee think thy swan a crow.

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: When the devout religion of mine eye

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Maintains such falsehood, then turn tears to fires;

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: And these, who often drown’d could never die,

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Transparent heretics, be burnt for liars!

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: One fairer than my love! the all-seeing sun

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: Ne’er saw her match since first the world begun.

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: Tut, you saw her fair, none else being by,

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: Herself poised with herself in either eye:

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: But in that crystal scales let there be weigh’d

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: Your lady’s love against some other maid

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: That I will show you shining at this feast,

lines.speaker: BENVOLIO
lines.text: And she shall scant show well that now shows best.

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: I’ll go along, no such sight to be shown,

lines.speaker: ROMEO
lines.text: But to rejoice in splendour of mine own.

glosses.definition: obligated by the Prince's order to keep the peace
reputation; esteem

glosses.phrase: my child is yet a stranger in the world
glosses.definition: Juliet is still a child; Capulet's first mention of her youth
Juliet is not yet fourteen — Capulet's stated age is historically plausible for Elizabethan noble betrothal but marks her as very young
ruined; spoiled by early marriage and child-bearing

glosses.phrase: Earth hath swallow’d all my hopes but she
glosses.definition: Capulet has lost his other children; Juliet is his only heir
heir to my estate; my hope on earth — with a Latin pun on 'terra' / landed property
take possession of; enjoy
form of address to inferiors, rougher than 'sir'
wait
find and invite these guests
bawdy punning — cobbler tools (last/yard) confused with tailor tools
at just the right moment
helped (archaic past tense)
a common herb used to stop bleeding and draw out poison
tortured — stretched on the rack
farewell; may you stay in good cheer
drink a cup of wine — casual invitation
spring freshly dressed; Shakespeare's personification of April
fresh young women
one beauty more
impartial; unbiased
weigh; compare
the balance of the eyes — seeing other beauties will reset his judgment

annotations.title: Capulet on his daughter — 'the hopeful lady of my earth'
annotations.body: Capulet's first description of Juliet is surprisingly tender: she is his 'hopeful lady of my earth,' the only surviving child in a family that has buried others. The Latinate pun on 'earth' (terra, estate) compresses the two things she is to him — the living daughter he loves and the material heir who will preserve the house. This helps explain the violence of 3.5: Capulet's rage against Juliet is not only patriarchal but dynastic. She is the only thing that keeps his line going. Whether that helps or hurts her situation, the play asks us to see both sides.

trials.prompt: Why is Juliet Capulet's only remaining child?
options: She was the only one her parents wanted
Capulet says 'Earth hath swallow'd all my hopes but she' — his other children have died
The Capulets never had other children
Her siblings have been sent away to school

trials.prompt: When Capulet tells Paris that Juliet is 'yet a stranger in the world,' he means:
options: She is still young and unmarried — not yet initiated into adult society
She does not know Paris personally
She is shy at public gatherings
